name: Reader Monad

x.formalRole: Models a computation that reads from a shared, read-only environment threaded implicitly.
x.compositionRule: Reader r a ≅ r → a; bind passes the same environment to each step; ask retrieves it.
laws: Satisfies the monad laws (it is function composition over a fixed argument)
x.encoding: A function from environment to value, or DI of an immutable config/context. Often replaced by partial application or explicit parameters.
x.useCases: Dependency injection of config / context without passing it through every signature explicitly.
misuseRisks: Using Reader where plain parameters or constructor injection are clearer
Smuggling mutable state in as the "environment"

def: A monad encoding read-only access to a shared environment, threading configuration/context implicitly through a computation.
problem: Many functions need the same config/context; passing it explicitly everywhere is noisy.
mechanism: Represents a computation as env → a; bind feeds the identical environment to each composed step, and ask exposes it. Effectively functional dependency injection.
benefits: Composes with other effects via transformers
limits: Stacking with other effects needs ReaderT
Overkill for one or two dependencies
failures: Treating it as mutable state (it is read-only)
whenNot: Imperative codebases with established DI containers
myths: "Reader is the only way to do DI in FP" — explicit args / partial application often suffice
